title: climate finance / transition risk / repo markets | Research Paper | Climate change, bank liquidity and systemic risk
summary: This ECB Working Paper examines how climate transition risks affect short-term funding costs in the European repo market using transaction-level data from 2019-2022. The authors find that banks with higher financed emissions (proxied by GHG emissions of borrowers) consistently pay higher repo rates, with a one standard deviation increase in financed emissions translating into repo rates 7-12% higher on average. The paper identifies this premium as a combination of a risk premium and an "inconvenience premium" reflecting dealer banks' sustainability preferences.
